sop.title: Adding New Kit in Fishbowl
sop.body:
## Purpose

Biannually we add new Kits to the website, in addition to having them added into Woocommerce they need to be added into Fishbowl

## Scope

For whoever is in charge of channel cohesion and product management

## Procedure

1. Log into Fishbowl
2. Navigate to Materials > Product

If Adding Individual Kits: 

1. In the top right corner click on the down arrow next to the “New” Button
2. Select New Kit
3. From there add the following information in general
	1. Kit = Kit SKU
	2. Kit Disc. = Product Title
	3. Price = Matched price on the website
4. In Details: add Kit Weight (Total sum weight of all kit items)
5. In Kit Items: Click on the green \+ button on the far Right side 
	1. Kit Item Type = Standard
	2. Kit Item Product = Base product (CANNOT BE ANOTHER KIT)
	3. Qty = how many bags of base product
	4. Finish
6. Hit Save in the Top Left corner.

If adding bulk Kits:

1. Go to File > Export in the very top left corner
2. Select “Kit Items”
3. Hit Next > Finish
4. Open the Spreadsheet
5. Using existing Kit item layouts mirror the format and add new Kits to the bottom of the spreadsheet. Keep in mind that each new kit needs two rows.
6. Once all of the information is added in Save the spreadsheet
7. In Fishbowl Navigate back to File > Import
8. Select Kit Items then Next > Next
9. Add in the correct file and Finish
10. Double check in products that all of the new Kits came in correctly.

## Exceptions / Special Notes

This process is for new KITS only. New products  cannot be added in this manor, there is a whole other SOP for how to add new Products. NEW PRODUCTS HAVE TO BE ADDED FIRST BEFORE YOU ADD THEIR KITS. When you add in new kits they should automatically add into Sellware when it automatically pushes every two hours. So don't be discouraged if they are not imported immediately. If you need to manually push out of Fishbowl go to Integrations > Commerce > Product Export > Run Task. And it should load in the next 5 minutes on sellware.
